review_text: Excellent colour pay off. Highly pigmented lipstick ,will cover darkest of dark lipsticks in a single swipe. Staying power is also good . It stays on for good 7-8 hours. The only drawback i noticed is that the consistency of this liquid lipstick is rather thick as compared to other liquid lipsticks available. It's somewhere between liquid to cream which is why it takes a little more time to dry after application. It also feels heavy on the lips. You'll always feel that you've applied something on the lips . Not everyone would like that feeling. Also it doesn't come off easily , i tried the normal micellar water but didn't help . An oil based makeup remover or a balm will do a good job of removing this lipstick. Talking about the shade , it's a proper light pink shade , with blue undertones. It will suit only light to medium complexions. It's not a very good shade for dusky complexions as it will make your face washes out. 4 stars for the color payoff and staying power. Overall it's a good one for the price but I'd say there are better lipsticks available in that same range , in the same brand . I honestly wouldn't repurchase any other shade from this range.

review_text: I have two shades from this range forever frappe and everlasting plum. For my NC 42 skin tone, forever frappe is the best nude. Being of a wheatish complexion, can mean most nudes would wash you out. But not forever frappe. With a single swipe it is a proper brownish nude with a very 90s vibe. With two swipes it actually takes on a more rusty earthy kind of tone, which is amazing if you are going heavier on the eyes. It's a very effortless shade and the staying power. Goodness gracious. It applies without tugging or pulling. It doesn't make the lips dry. Doesn't make them parched. Glides on like a creamy lipstick and makes your lips quite smooth giving it a hydrated feel. But it doesn't budge. It just doesn't budge. It has stayed on my lips for more than five hours and it has been intact. Now coming to the other shade, everlasting plum, it is a very on trend fall berry.

review_title: Best try if you have never tried highlights before
review_text: I have got knee length long hair and naturally smooth and straight hair but my hair is very thin so I was always scared of hair colouring. So I gave this highlight a try only coloured my front two locks to see how it goes, and trust me its amazing. If you are scared of bleach, then u can go for this product as it is not harsh at all, it doesn't burn the colour of your hair like bleach does it maintains the smooth texture of natural hair. This particular colour isn't vibrant but a ver subtle sunkissed brown which looks really very descent. I can assure you guys for a safe try because it is amazing and far better than other hair colors which u can do at home like streax. For best results, don't use the applicator given with the pack, instead use hair colouring brush take strips of desired hair and paint and wrap in a foil paper keep it for around 1 and a half hours and give some heat with a blow dryer. Remember: if your hair is previously chemical or colour treated this product may not develop the colour on you, especially if you have black coloured your hair.

review_title: Alcol denat. and Alpha-Isomethyl Ionone are icky ingredients
review_text: Alpha-Isomethyl Ionone is a common fragrance ingredient that is one of the ‚ÄúEU 26 fragrances‚Äù that has to be labelled separately (and cannot be simply included in the term ‚Äúfragrance/perfume‚Äù on the label) because of allergen potential. Best to avoid if your skin is sensitive. If you want to start a debate between skincare experts, mentioning alcohol will probably be a good way to do that (Btw, denat means denaturated and it's normal alcohol with some additives that make it poisonous and bad tasting so that people do not drink it.). It's a controversial ingredient that has definitely many immediate skincare benefits and some potential longer-term drawbacks. So let's see first the benefits, why it's so often used. First, it is a great solvent. There are some ingredients that do not dissolve in water or oil and alcohol is often the way to go. Second, it's great at creating cosmetically elegant, fast absorbing, thin formulas. For example creating a nice, non-oily sunscreen is still a big challenge to the cosmetic industry (because sunscreen ingredients are yucky and oily) and alcohol can help with this problem. Third, alcohol is a great astringent, leaves a refreshing feeling on the skin so it's very popular as a toner ingredient. And last but not least, it's antimicrobial, helps to disinfect the skin so it is common in after-shaves or products intended for acne-prone skin. It even works as a preservative that is often used in natural products. So now let's see what's not so good about it: First, it dries the skin. As an immediate effect for oily skin types that might sound like a benefit, but in the longer term alcohol often dries out the skin so much that the skin compensates with extra oil production. Famous dermatologist, Dr. Leslie Baumann in her Skin Type Solution book suggests avoiding alcohol if it's in the first 7 positions on the ingredient list. Skin care expert, Paula Begoun thinks that alcohol not only dries the skin but regular exposure to it damages the skin barrier and causes inflammation. Not everyone agrees with this. Nicki Zevola from Futurederm thinks that alcohol evaporates quickly from the skin, can act as a penetration enhancer for key ingredients and is safe to use. So the bottom line is that alcohol (and to further complicate we are talking only about simple, volatile, low molecular weight alcohols here) is debated. What seems to be a fact is that it's drying but it also has several benefits. Based on that it's up to you to decide if it's something to avoid or not. SO HUGE NO TO THIS ONE.
